_source: film emulsion specs transcribed from official manufacturer datasheets, authored in data/curated-film-stocks/*.jsonl; each record carries its own datasheetUrl
_license: individual specs are non-copyrightable facts; our own compilation built from primary datasheets

stocks.name: CHS 100 II
stocks.notes: Classic single-layer emulsion made from two emulsions; sensitization optimized for greyscale separation and large exposure latitude. Coated on clear archival PET (100 micron for 135 and 120; 175 micron for sheetfilm). Can be reversal processed. AHU plus backside AH/NC layer in 135 and 120; backside AH/NC layer only in sheetfilm. Made in Germany. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

stocks.name: CMS 20 II
stocks.notes: Ultra-high-resolution monodisperse emulsion resolving up to 800 lp/mm. Rated 20 ASA in dedicated ADOTECH developer (pictorial); up to 80 ASA for high contrast; 3-6 ASA in non-dedicated low-contrast developers. AHU anti-halation layer between emulsion and base; totally transparent base. Reciprocity: +1/2 stop at 1s and at 1/1000s. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

stocks.name: HR-50
stocks.notes: High-resolution film based on a technical emulsion; speed boosted to 50 ASA (ISO 50/17°) via ADOX SPEED BOOST technology, gamma reduced for pictorial use. Resolution up to 280 lp/mm at 1000:1 image contrast. Base 0.1mm PET; combined AHU/NC anti-halation. Usable as an infrared film and responds strongly to filtration. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

stocks.name: Scala 160
stocks.notes: Black and white reversal (slide) film designed specifically for reversal processing at 160 ASA; dedicated ADOX SCALA reversal kit available. Coated on 120 micron clear triacetate with increased silver content, yielding DMAX of 3.6 or higher. Anti-halation layer between emulsion and base. Not DX coded. When shot as a negative, expose at 80-100 ASA. Reciprocity: +1/2 from 1s, +1 from 10s, +2 from 100s. No standalone PDF; specifications published on official ADOX product page.

stocks.name: Scala 50
stocks.notes: High-resolution B&W slide film based on a technical emulsion; speed boosted to 50 ASA (ISO 50/17°) with gamma reduced for pictorial use. Resolution up to 280 lp/mm at 1000:1 image contrast. Base 0.1mm PET; combined AHU/NC anti-halation. Processed as a slide in the dedicated ADOX SCALA reversal kit; can also be processed as a negative using HR-50 times. Usable as an infrared film. No standalone PDF; specifications published on official ADOX product page.

stocks.name: Silvermax 100
stocks.notes: Currently discontinued (ADOX recommends CHS 100 II / Scala 50 as alternatives). APX 100 recipe with finer grain, slightly lower speed and ~30% higher silver content. Achieves up to 14 stops copy range in dedicated Silvermax developer. Coated on 120 micron clear triacetate; anti-halation layer between emulsion and base; can optionally be reversal-processed to a B&W slide (ADOX Scala). Not DX coded. Reciprocity: +1/2 from 1s, +1 from 10s, +2 from 100s. Made in Germany. Datasheet hosted by Fotoimpex, authored by ADOX Fotowerke GmbH.

stocks.name: PAN-X Reverso Super 8
stocks.notes: True B&W Super 8 reversal film, projectable right after development; positioned as a replacement for the discontinued Kodak Plus-X. Currently unavailable. Silver-rich emulsion with full shadow detail at 100 ASA and DMAX above 3.0; anti-halation layer between base and emulsion; clear triacetate base. 15 meters (~3 min 20 s). Standard developing in Kodak D-94A process; expose at 64 or 100 ASA (best results at 64 ASA); cartridge notched 100 ASA Tungsten. No standalone PDF; specifications published on official ADOX product page.

stocks.name: 50D
stocks.notes: Daylight-balanced (5500K) color negative motion picture emulsion (Kodak 50D-derived), remjet removed for safe C-41 processing. EI 50/18deg; usable EI 12-100 without push. World's finest grain color negative film per manufacturer. Acetate base with halation effect.

stocks.name: 400D
stocks.notes: 400Dynamic. Daylight-balanced color negative motion picture emulsion, remjet removed for C-41. Base ISO 400, rateable 200-800 without push, push up to 3 stops. Process-surviving anti-static lubricant coating. Halation at overexposed highlights.

stocks.name: 800T
stocks.notes: 800Tungsten. Tungsten-balanced color negative motion picture emulsion, remjet (remjet backing) removed for safe C-41 photo-lab processing. Push up to 3200 ISO. Acetate base; characteristic halation. Also processable in ECN-2/Cs2.

stocks.name: BwXX
stocks.notes: Rebranded Kodak Double-X 5222 motion picture B&W stock. Panchromatic. Variable base sensitivity: ISO 250 daylight (5500K) / ISO 200 tungsten (3200K); rateable up to EI 1600 with push. Acetate base, DX-coded 135.

stocks.name: Color Negative 100
stocks.notes: Color Negative 100. Standard C-41. Extra-fine grain, low ISO, vivid colors. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

stocks.name: Color Negative 400
stocks.notes: Color Negative 400. Standard C-41. Fine grain, versatile. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

stocks.name: Color Negative 800
stocks.notes: Color Negative 800. Standard C-41. High-speed, good low-light performance. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

stocks.name: Lady Grey 400
stocks.notes: Lady Grey B&W ISO 400, 35 mm. Rebranded emulsion (re-spooled other manufacturer's B&W emulsion); Lomography does not name the parent stock.

stocks.name: Earl Grey 100
stocks.notes: Earl Grey B&W ISO 100, 35 mm. Rebranded emulsion (re-spooled other manufacturer's B&W emulsion); Lomography does not name the parent stock.

stocks.name: Berlin Kino 400
stocks.notes: Berlin Kino B&W ISO 400. Panchromatic cine emulsion; pushable to 800/1600/3200 per manufacturer. Suited to color filters. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

stocks.name: Potsdam Kino 100
stocks.notes: Potsdam Kino B&W ISO 100. Triacetate-based B&W cine emulsion, medium speed, heavy silver-halide composition. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

stocks.name: Fantome Kino 8
stocks.notes: Fantome Kino B&W ISO 8, 35 mm. High-contrast B&W cine emulsion. Part of Lomography Kino B&W film family.

stocks.name: LomoChrome Metropolis
stocks.notes: 2021 LomoChrome Metropolis. Experimental color-shift color negative film, extended EI 100-400, standard C-41. Punchy contrast, desaturated muted tones with pops of color. Offered in 35 mm, 120, 110.

stocks.name: LomoChrome Purple
stocks.notes: 2021 LomoChrome Purple Petillant. Color-shift color negative film (green-to-purple infrared-like shift), extended EI 100-400, standard C-41. Offered in 35 mm, 120, 110.

stocks.name: LomoChrome Turquoise
stocks.notes: 2021 LomoChrome Turquoise. Color-shift color negative film, extended EI 100-400, standard C-41 (non-DX-coded, 36 exp). Fine grain. Offered in 35 mm, 120, 110.

stocks.name: Redscale XR 50-200
stocks.notes: Redscale XR. Redscale color negative film (film wound emulsion-reversed), extended EI 50-200, standard C-41. Glowing red/orange/yellow tones. Offered in 35 mm and 120.

stocks.name: Fomapan 100
stocks.notes: Fomapan 100 Classic. RMS 13.5 (Microphen 20C, gamma 0.6, D=1.0). 135 uses gray/gray-blue cellulose triacetate base; 120 and sheet use clear polyester base with antihalo backing. Wide latitude: usable at EI 50-400. Can also be reversal-processed.

stocks.name: Fomapan 200
stocks.notes: Fomapan 200 Creative. T-crystal emulsion for high resolution and low granularity. RMS 14.0 (Microphen 20C, gamma 0.6, D=1.0). 135 base is gray/gray-blue cellulose triacetate; 120 and sheet use clear polyester base with antihalo backing. Wide latitude EI 100-800.

stocks.name: Fomapan 400
stocks.notes: Fomapan 400 Action. RMS 17.5 (Microphen 20C, gamma 0.6, D=1.0). 135 base is gray/gray-blue cellulose triacetate; 120 and sheet use clear polyester base with antihalo backing. Wide latitude EI 200-1600. Can also be reversal-processed.

stocks.name: Retropan 320 Soft
stocks.notes: Retropan 320 soft. Special negative film with wide range of half tones and soft light. Wide latitude EI 160-1250. Recommended developer: Retro Special Developer. 135 base is gray/gray-blue cellulose triacetate; 120 and sheet use clear polyester base with antihalo backing. Sheet film offered in various sizes (box of 50). Datasheet gives no numeric resolving power or RMS granularity value.

stocks.name: Fomapan R100
stocks.notes: Fomapan R (Fomapan R100) black-and-white reversal film. ISO 100/21 when processed in R-100 Process. RMS 13.0 (measured at gamma 1.1, Process R-100). Cellulose triacetate safety base 0.125 mm. Supplied as 135-36 and 16mm cine (single/double perf, 2x8 Standard, DS8 super).

stocks.name: Ortho 400
stocks.notes: FOMA Ortho 400. Orthochromatic; high green sensitivity. RMS 17.5 (Microphen 20C, gamma 0.6, D=1.0). Wide latitude EI 160-1600. 120 uses bluish polyester base without antihalo layer; 135 uses gray/gray-blue cellulose triacetate; sheet uses clear polyester with antihalo backing. Can be handled briefly under 585nm+ (orange) safelight.

stocks.name: Acros 100 II
stocks.notes: ISO 100/21. Orthopanchromatic. Base: TAC (cellulose triacetate), gray base, 0.134 mm (135). Diffuse RMS granularity value 7 (processed in Microfine, 48 um aperture, density 1.0 above D-min). Resolving power 200 lp/mm at 1000:1, 60 lp/mm at 1.6:1. Datasheet AF3-0258E is the 135 sheet; a separate 120 datasheet exists at asset.fujifilm.com. grainRms field holds Fuji diffuse RMS granularity value as published (not RMS x10).

stocks.name: Provia 100F
stocks.notes: FUJICHROME PROVIA 100F Professional [RDP III], daylight-type ISO 100/21. Process E-6 (also CR-56/C6R). Base: cellulose triacetate. Diffuse RMS granularity value 8 (48 um aperture, density 1.0 above D-min). Resolving power 140 lp/mm at 1000:1, 60 lp/mm at 1.6:1. Also QuickLoad 4x5 and 11x14 sheet in some markets. grainRms holds Fuji diffuse RMS granularity value as published.

stocks.name: Velvia 50
stocks.notes: FUJICHROME Velvia 50 Professional [RVP50], daylight-type ISO 50/18. Process E-6 (also CR-56/PR06). Base: cellulose triacetate for rolls, polyester for sheets. Diffuse RMS granularity value 9 (48 um aperture, density 1.0 above D-min). Resolving power 160 lp/mm at 1000:1, 80 lp/mm at 1.6:1. grainRms holds Fuji diffuse RMS granularity value as published.

stocks.name: Velvia 100
stocks.notes: FUJICHROME Velvia 100 Professional [RVP100], daylight-type ISO 100/21. Process E-6 (also CR-56). Base: cellulose triacetate for rolls, polyester for sheets. Diffuse RMS granularity value 8 (48 um aperture, density 1.0 above D-min). Resolving power 160 lp/mm at 1000:1, 80 lp/mm at 1.6:1. grainRms holds Fuji diffuse RMS granularity value as published.

stocks.name: Fujicolor C200
stocks.notes: FUJICOLOR C200, daylight-type ISO 200/24. Process C-41 (also Fujifilm CN-16 series). Base: cellulose triacetate, 122 um (135). Diffuse RMS granularity value 4 (48 um aperture, density 1.0 above D-min; Fuji notes color-negative RMS not comparable to reversal film). Resolving power 125 lp/mm at 1000:1, 50 lp/mm at 1.6:1. Datasheet AF3-0249E. grainRms holds Fuji diffuse RMS granularity value as published.

stocks.name: Superia X-TRA 400
stocks.notes: FUJICOLOR SUPERIA X-TRA 400 [CH], daylight-type ISO 400/27. Process C-41 (also Fujifilm CN-16 series). Base: cellulose triacetate, 122 um (135). Diffuse RMS granularity value 4 (48 um aperture, density 1.0 above D-min; Fuji notes not comparable to reversal film). Resolving power 125 lp/mm at 1000:1, 50 lp/mm at 1.6:1. Datasheet AF3-0217E. grainRms holds Fuji diffuse RMS granularity value as published.

stocks.name: Pro 400H
stocks.notes: FUJICOLOR PRO 400H PROFESSIONAL, daylight-type ISO 400/27, 4th color layer. Discontinued 2021; official archived datasheet AF3-176E. Process C-41 (also Fujifilm CN-16). Base: cellulose triacetate, 122 um (135) / 98 um (120,220). Diffuse RMS granularity value 4 (48 um aperture, density +1.0 above D-min; Fuji notes not comparable to reversal film). Resolving power 125 lp/mm at 1000:1, 50 lp/mm at 1.6:1. grainRms holds Fuji diffuse RMS granularity value as published.

stocks.name: instax mini
stocks.notes: Instant film, no user development. Hi-Speed ISO 800 (per official Fujifilm/Instax product spec). Credit-card size film 5.4 x 8.6 cm; image (picture) size 62 x 46 mm (per instax mini camera spec). 10 exposures per pack. Emulsion rated 5C to 40C. Fujifilm does not publish an engineering datasheet (no RMS/resolving-power figures) for instant film; ISO and sizes are from official product/spec pages, not a datasheet PDF.

stocks.name: instax SQUARE
stocks.notes: Instant film, no user development. Hi-Speed ISO 800 (per official Fujifilm/Instax product spec). Film size 86 x 72 mm; image (picture) size 62 x 62 mm. 10 exposures per pack. Fujifilm does not publish an engineering datasheet (no RMS/resolving-power figures) for instant film; ISO and sizes are from official product/spec pages, not a datasheet PDF.

stocks.name: instax WIDE
stocks.notes: Instant film, no user development. Hi-Speed ISO 800 (per official Fujifilm/Instax product spec). Film size 10.6 x 8.4 cm; image (picture) size approx 99 x 62 mm. 10 exposures per pack. Emulsion rated 5C to 40C. Fujifilm does not publish an engineering datasheet (no RMS/resolving-power figures) for instant film; ISO and sizes are from official product/spec pages, not a datasheet PDF.

stocks.name: HP5 Plus
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 400/27 box speed; usable at meter settings EI 400/27 to EI 3200/36 with extended development
stocks.notes: ISO 400/27. 35mm on 0.125mm acetate; 120 on 0.110mm acetate; sheet on 0.180mm polyester. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.31. Datasheet Nov 2018.

stocks.name: FP4 Plus
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 125/22 box speed; good image quality EI 50/18 to EI 200/24; tolerates +6 stops over / -2 stops under
stocks.notes: ISO 125/22 (measured in ID-11). 35mm on 0.125mm acetate; 120 on 0.110mm acetate; sheet on 0.180mm polyester. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.26. Datasheet Nov 2018.

stocks.name: Pan F Plus
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 50/18 box speed; good image quality also at EI 25/15
stocks.notes: ISO 50/18 (measured in ID-11). 35mm on 0.125mm acetate; 120 on 0.110mm acetate. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.33. Process within 3 months of exposure. Datasheet Dec 2018.

stocks.name: Delta 100
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 100/21 box speed; good image quality EI 50/18 to EI 200/24
stocks.notes: ISO 100/21. Full product name Delta 100 Professional. 35mm on 0.125mm acetate; 120 on 0.110mm acetate; sheet on 0.180mm polyester. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.26. Datasheet Apr 2023.

stocks.name: Delta 400
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 400/27 box speed; good image quality EI 200/24 to EI 3200/36 with extended development
stocks.notes: ISO 400/27 (measured in ID-11). Full product name Delta 400 Professional. 35mm on 0.125mm acetate; 120 on 0.110mm acetate. Normal contrast Gbar 0.62. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.41. Datasheet Nov 2018.

stocks.name: Delta 3200
stocks.exposureLatitude: Designed for EI 3200/36; good image quality EI 400/27 to EI 6400/39; usable up to EI 25000/45
stocks.notes: Box EI 3200/36; measured ISO speed 1000/31 to daylight (ID-11). Full product name Delta 3200 Professional. 35mm on 0.125mm acetate; 120 roll film. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.33. Datasheet Jun 2025.

stocks.name: XP2 Super
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 400/27 box speed; usable EI 50/18 to EI 800/30; finer grain when overexposed/downrated
stocks.notes: ISO 400/27 chromogenic black-and-white film processed in standard C41. 35mm on 0.125mm acetate; 120 on 0.110mm acetate. Push processing not recommended. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.31. Datasheet Nov 2018.

stocks.name: SFX 200
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 200/24 box speed; extended red sensitivity up to 740nm
stocks.notes: ISO 200/24 (measured in ID-11). Extended red sensitivity to 740nm plus full panchromatic sensitivity. Coated on 0.125mm grey acetate base. Datasheet Nov 2018.

stocks.name: Ortho Plus
stocks.exposureLatitude: ISO 80/20 daylight, ISO 40/17 tungsten; 135 cassettes DX coded ISO 80
stocks.notes: Orthochromatic (blue/green sensitive, no red). ISO 80/20 daylight, ISO 40/17 tungsten. 35mm and 120 on 0.125mm acetate; sheet on 0.180mm polyester. Deep-red safelight usable (ILFORD 906). Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.25. Datasheet Oct 2019.

stocks.name: Kentmere Pan 100
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 100/21 box speed; development times given for EI 50/18 to EI 200/24
stocks.notes: ISO 100/21 (measured in ID-11). Coated on 0.125mm acetate base; 135 and 120. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.26. Datasheet Jul 2022.

stocks.name: Kentmere Pan 400
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 400/27 box speed; development times given for EI 320/26 to EI 800/30
stocks.notes: ISO 400/27 (measured in ID-11). Coated on 0.125mm acetate base; 135 and 120. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.30. Datasheet Jul 2022.

stocks.name: Harman Phoenix 200
stocks.exposureLatitude: ISO 200/24 box speed; best results EI 100-200, many users prefer EI 160
stocks.notes: Experimental ISO 200/24 C41 colour negative. High contrast, strong grain, no orange mask, limited antihalation (halation effects). Coated on 0.125mm acetate; 135 and 120. Push processing not recommended. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.31. Datasheet Sep 2024.

stocks.name: Harman Phoenix II
stocks.exposureLatitude: ISO 200/24 box speed; best results EI 100-200; wider latitude than original Phoenix
stocks.notes: 2nd-generation experimental ISO 200/24 C41 colour negative. More normal contrast/colour, finer grain than Phoenix 200; no orange mask. Coated on 0.125mm acetate; 135 and 120. Push processing not recommended. Reciprocity Ta=Tm^1.31. Datasheet Jul 2025.

stocks.name: Harman Red 125
stocks.exposureLatitude: EI 125 nominal; usable range EI 50-400; best results EI 100-200
stocks.notes: C41-process redscale film (name on datasheet: HARMAN Red). EI 125/22 daylight (practical evaluation). Emulsion reversed/outermost - reverse orientation for processing. Coated on 0.125mm acetate; DX coded ISO 125; 135 and 120. Push processing not recommended. Datasheet Jun 2025.

stocks.name: Tri-X 400
stocks.reciprocity: No correction 1/1000 to 1/10 s; at 1 s +1 stop, at 10 s +2 stops, at 100 s +3 stops
stocks.notes: F-4017; 400TX; contrast index 0.56 dev target; resolving power at TOC 1000:1

stocks.name: Tri-X 320
stocks.notes: F-4017; 320TXP; sheet film on 7-mil Estar Thick Base; excellent tone gradation

stocks.name: T-Max 100
stocks.reciprocity: No correction 1/1000 to 1/10 s; at 1 s +1/3 stop, at 10 s +1/2 stop, at 100 s +1 stop
stocks.notes: F-4016; 100TMX; T-GRAIN emulsion; 63 lp/mm at TOC 1.6:1, 200 lp/mm at TOC 1000:1

stocks.name: T-Max 400
stocks.reciprocity: No correction 1/10000 to 1 s; at 10 s +1/3 stop, at 100 s +1 1/2 stops
stocks.notes: F-4043; 400TMY; T-GRAIN emulsion; 50 lp/mm at TOC 1.6:1, 200 lp/mm at TOC 1000:1

stocks.name: T-Max P3200
stocks.notes: F-4001; 3200TMZ; multi-speed, nominal EI 800 (EI 1000 in T-MAX dev); usable EI 400-25000; 40 lp/mm at TOC 1.6:1, 125 lp/mm at TOC 1000:1

stocks.name: Portra 160
stocks.notes: E-4051; codes 5059/6059/4059; sheets on Estar Thick Base; grain reported as Print Grain Index (28 at 4x6/135), not RMS

stocks.name: Portra 400
stocks.notes: E-4050; codes 5056/6056/4056; sheets on Estar Thick Base; grain reported as Print Grain Index (37 at 4x6/135), not RMS

stocks.name: Portra 800
stocks.notes: E-4040; best-in-class underexposure latitude; grain reported as Print Grain Index (48 at 4x6/135), not RMS

stocks.name: Ektar 100
stocks.notes: E-4046; codes 5110/6110; sheets on Estar Thick Base; finest-grain color negative; grain reported as Print Grain Index (<25 at 4x6/135), not RMS

stocks.name: Ektachrome E100
stocks.reciprocity: No correction 1/10000 to 10 s; at 120 s add CC10R
stocks.notes: E-4000; color transparency film; T-GRAIN; RMS granularity 8 (read at gross diffuse visual density 1.0)

stocks.name: Gold 200
stocks.notes: E-7022; grain reported as Print Grain Index (44 at 4x6/135), not RMS

stocks.name: UltraMax 400
stocks.notes: E-7023; KODAK ULTRA MAX 400; T-GRAIN; grain reported as Print Grain Index (46 at 4x6/135), not RMS

stocks.name: Double-X 5222
stocks.notes: H-1-5222; EASTMAN DOUBLE-X 5222 (35mm)/7222 (16mm); grey acetate base; EI 250 daylight / 200 tungsten; 32 lp/mm at TOC 1.6:1, 100 lp/mm at TOC 1000:1; process D-96

stocks.name: Vision3 50D
stocks.notes: H-1-5203; 5203 (35mm)/7203 (16mm); daylight-balanced EI 50 (12 tungsten w/80A); acetate base with rem-jet backing

stocks.name: Vision3 250D
stocks.notes: H-1-5207; 5207 (35mm)/7207 (16mm); daylight-balanced EI 250 (64 tungsten w/80A); acetate base with rem-jet backing

stocks.name: Vision3 200T
stocks.notes: H-1-5213; 5213 (35mm)/7213 (16mm); tungsten-balanced EI 200 (125 daylight w/85); acetate base with rem-jet backing

stocks.name: Vision3 500T
stocks.notes: H-1-5219; 5219 (35mm)/7219 (16mm); tungsten-balanced EI 500 (320 daylight w/85); acetate base with rem-jet backing

stocks.name: Verita 200D
stocks.notes: H-1-5206; 5206 (35mm/65mm)/7206 (16mm); daylight-balanced EI 200; acetate base (no rem-jet); shorter dynamic range than Vision3; motion-picture only, no still format

stocks.name: Ektapan 100
stocks.notes: T-Grain B&W; the renamed KODAK T-MAX 100. Cat# 135-36 7519556, 35mm-100ft 7520885, 120 7519630, 4x5 7520828/7520836. Image-structure figures per T-Max 100 (F-4016).

stocks.name: Ektapan 400
stocks.notes: T-Grain B&W; the renamed KODAK T-MAX 400. Cat# 135-36 7519564, 35mm-100ft 7520877, 120 7519622, 4x5 7520778/7520810. Image-structure figures per T-Max 400 (F-4043).

stocks.name: Ektapan P3200
stocks.notes: Multi-speed T-Grain push film (box P3200, nominal ISO ~800); the renamed KODAK T-MAX P3200. Cat# 135-36 7519572. See T-Max P3200 (F-4001).

stocks.name: Ektacolor Pro 160
stocks.notes: C-41; the renamed KODAK PORTRA 160. Cat# 135-36 7519549, 120 7519598, 4x5 7520794, 8x10 7523137. Portra uses Print Grain Index (not RMS); see E-4051.

stocks.name: Ektacolor Pro 400
stocks.notes: C-41; the renamed KODAK PORTRA 400. Available in 135 and 120. See Portra 400 (E-4050).

stocks.name: Ektacolor Pro 800
stocks.notes: C-41; the renamed KODAK PORTRA 800. Cat# 135-36 7519531, 120 7519614. See Portra 800 (E-4040).

stocks.name: Kodacolor 100
stocks.notes: C-41 consumer color negative; the renamed KODAK PRO IMAGE 100 (2026 Eastman Kodak consumer rebrand). No standalone PDF datasheet published; the product page is the official reference. Verified 135/36exp; other formats not confirmed.

stocks.name: Kodacolor 200
stocks.notes: C-41 consumer color negative; the renamed KODAK COLORPLUS 200 (2026 Eastman Kodak consumer rebrand). No standalone PDF datasheet published; the product page is the official reference. Verified 135/36exp.

stocks.name: UN54
stocks.notes: ORWO Universal Negative Film UN 54. Panchromatic medium-speed B&W negative camera film; can be reversal-processed (ORWO instruction 4185) for direct positives. Grey triacetate safety base 135 um. RMS granularity 12 (net diffuse density 1.0, 48 um aperture). MTF factor m30 = 0.80. Negative processing per ORWO instruction 1182 (D96) to avg gradient 0.65. Also sold in 135/36exp as Wolfen UN54 (https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/wolfen-un54). Manufacturer: Filmotec GmbH, Wolfen.

stocks.name: NC500
stocks.notes: Wolfen NC500 color negative film. Cinema negative stock without remjet backing, so develops in standard C-41; ECN2 gives a flatter gradation. 125 um grey triacetate base. ISO 400/27. Daylight-balanced; densitometry status M. Coated by InovisCoat GmbH. Product page: https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/orwo-nc500-colour-cine-35mm

stocks.name: NC400
stocks.notes: Wolfen NC400 color negative film. Kodak C-41 process. 125 um grey triacetate base. ISO 400/27. Daylight-balanced; densitometry status M. Coated by InovisCoat GmbH. Product pages: https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/wolfen-nc400-36exp and https://www.orwo.shop/en-us/products/orwo-16mm-nc400-colour-cine

stocks.name: P30
stocks.notes: FERRANIA P30. 80 ISO panchromatic B&W motion-picture-heritage film for still photography, high silver content, ultrafine grain. Shoot at box speed 80 ISO; not DX coded. Traditional B&W chemistry (best-practices PDF lists D-76, D-96, HC-110, Rodinal, XTOL). Best Practices PDF: https://www.filmferrania.com/s/P30_Best_Practices_v3.pdf . Base not stated on official sources; omitted. Sourced from filmferrania.com (the .it domain has lapsed and now serves unrelated content).

stocks.name: Orto
stocks.notes: Ferrania Orto. 50 ISO orthochromatic B&W film, high silver content, practically imperceptible grain. New product developed with modern chemicals for a classic orthochromatic (blue-sensitive) aesthetic. Crafted in Cairo Montenotte, Italy. Official product page confirms 50 ISO / 120 / orthochromatic; base not stated. Sourced from filmferrania.com.

stocks.name: Mono 100
stocks.notes: Kosmo Foto Mono. 100-ISO panchromatic B&W film. Traditional B&W chemistry (not C-41 minilab compatible); can pull to ISO 50 or push to ISO 400. Rebadged emulsion from a European film producer, factory-fresh. 120 version at https://kosmofoto.com/product/kosmo-foto-mono-120-film/ . Base not officially stated.

stocks.name: Agent Shadow 400
stocks.notes: Kosmo Foto Agent Shadow. 400-ISO panchromatic B&W film for 35mm. Push-processable four stops to ISO 6400. Fine-grained at box speed, more atmospheric grain and dramatic contrast when pushed. Existing emulsion from a company with over a century of film-making experience. Base not officially stated.

stocks.name: Pancro 400
stocks.notes: BERGGER Pancro 400. Two-emulsion panchromatic B&W film (silver bromide + silver iodide, differing grain sizes) for wide exposure latitude; nominal 400 ISO, usable 160-1600. Classical grain, variable with developer. Sheets coated on 175 um PET base with undercoated anti-halation layer and anti-curling layer. Datasheet PDF: https://bergger.com/en/index.php?controller=attachment&id_attachment=4 . 135 page: https://bergger.com/en/films-sheets/bergger-pancro-400-35-mm.html ; 120 page: https://bergger.com/en/films-sheets/bergger-pancro-400-rouleau-120.html

stocks.name: Washi S
stocks.notes: Film Washi 'S' - 50 iso/18 - motion-picture sound-recording film. Polyester base 120 um; anti-halation layer placed between base and emulsion (defining feature). Panchromatic (max 620 nm; not fully red-sensitive). Fine grain, very strong contrast, industrial coating. Also offered on 30.5m bulk roll. Discontinued per Washi.

stocks.name: Washi F
stocks.notes: Film Washi 'F' - 100 iso/21 - medical fluorographic X-ray film. Polyester base 100 um, coated without anti-halation layer (high diffusion/halation effect). Orthochromatic (max 575 nm; red safelight). High grain, medium contrast, hand-coated.

stocks.name: Washi D
stocks.notes: Film Washi 'D' - 500 iso/28 - aerial surveillance/cartography film. Very thin polyester base 75 um. Panchromatic (max 690 nm; process in total darkness). Pushable to 1600; can also be processed as slide. Moderate grain, strong contrast, industrial coating. Offered in 36exp, 72exp, single-use camera, and 30.5m bulk. 135 production paused/discontinued per Washi.

stocks.name: Washi W
stocks.notes: Film Washi 'W' - 25 iso/15 - orthochromatic emulsion hand-coated on Kozo paper (28 g/m2, Moriki Paper Tosa Washi), medium transparency. Orthochromatic (max 545 nm; sensitive to blue, less green, no red; red 575 nm safelight). Datasheet gives usable range ~3/6 to 12/25 iso depending on developer/light; box max sensitivity 25 iso. Very strong texture and contrast, hand-coated.

stocks.name: Washi X
stocks.notes: Film Washi 'X' - 100 iso/21 - maskless color film (no orange mask). Per Washi's official product listing it can be processed in C-41 (negative) or E6 (slide). No dedicated datasheet PDF is published; only the product-listing text is authoritative. Base not stated. (Note: this is a 100-iso maskless color film, not a panchromatic BW stock.)

stocks.name: Washi V
stocks.notes: Film Washi 'V' - 100 iso/21 - panchromatic emulsion hand-coated on Gampi paper (41 g/m2, Awagami Factory, Japan), high transparency. Panchromatic (max 640 nm; process in full darkness). Push/pull +/-2 stops. Medium texture, soft contrast, hand-coated. Production stopped (Gampi paper supply); discontinued per Washi.

stocks.name: X Film 80
stocks.notes: CatLABS X FILM 80 MKII B&W negative film. Traditional fine-grain B&W film in the vein of Kodak Panatomic-X; very fine grain, moderate contrast, wide latitude, high silver content. IE 80 (expose at 80-100; rate 50 indoors; push to 800 in low light). Offered in 120 and 4x5/8x10 sheets. Base and spectral sensitivity not explicitly stated on official page.

stocks.name: X Film 320
stocks.notes: CatLABS X FILM 320 Pro B&W negative. EI 320 nominal (rate 200 in bright sun; push to 1600 in low light). Extended red / near-IR sensitivity to ~750 nm (use R72 IR filter at ISO 3-5 for IR effect). Exceptionally fine grain, extremely clear base. Non-DX. Offered in 35mm and 120. 35mm variant listing: https://www.catlabs.info/product/xpan320 . Base not stated.

stocks.name: RPX 25
stocks.notes: Low-sensitivity panchromatic B&W film, ISO 25/15deg. Crystal-clear 100 micron polyester base. Layer thickness 5 micron, high silver content. Resolving power 260 lp/mm at 1000:1 contrast, RMS granularity 8 (x1000). Exposure latitude +/-1 f-stop. Suitable for B&W reversal.

stocks.name: RPX 100
stocks.notes: Medium-speed panchromatic B&W negative film, ISO 100/21deg. Triacetate base (120 micron) with light mask. Layer thickness 7 micron. Resolving power 160 lp/mm at 1000:1 contrast, RMS grain size 7 (x1000). Exposure latitude +/-2 f-stops. Pull-push 50-200 ISO. Suitable for B&W reversal.

stocks.name: RPX 400
stocks.notes: Highly sensitive panchromatic B&W film, ISO 400/27deg, spectral sensitivity 380-660 nm. Triacetate carrier with light mask. Layer thickness 10 micron. Resolving power 110 lp/mm at 1000:1 contrast, RMS granularity 14 (x1000). Exposure latitude +/-2 f-stops. Push-pull ISO 200-3200. Suitable for B&W reversal.

stocks.name: Retro 80S
stocks.notes: Superpanchromatic B&W film, ISO 80/20deg with extended red range up to 750 nm. Crystal-clear transparent PET base (usable as B&W slide film). Layer thickness 10 micron. Resolving power 180 lp/mm at 1000:1 contrast, RMS granularity 14 (x1000). Lower blue sensitivity. Extremely suitable for B&W reversal.

stocks.name: Retro 400S
stocks.notes: Highly sensitive panchromatic B&W negative film, ISO 400/27deg, spectral sensitivity 380-730 nm (extended red). Crystal-clear transparent PET base (usable as B&W slide film). Coating thickness 10 micron. Resolving power 160 lp/mm at 1000:1 contrast, RMS granularity 11 (x1000). Exposure latitude +/-2 f-stops, pushable to ISO 800/30deg. Very suitable for B&W reversal.

stocks.name: Superpan 200
stocks.notes: High-sensitivity superpanchromatic B&W negative film, ISO 200/24deg +/-1 f-stop, pushable to ISO 400/27deg. Extended infrared range up to 750 nm (usable as IR film with IR filter). PET base 100 micron (usable as B&W slide film). Layer thickness 10 micron. Resolving power 180 lp/mm, RMS grain size 14 (x1000). Wide exposure latitude (125-250 ISO). Extremely suitable for B&W reversal.

stocks.name: Infrared 400
stocks.notes: Hyperpanchromatically sensitised B&W film, nominal sensitivity ISO 200/24deg to 400/27deg, IR range 650-750 nm (IR filter recommended 715-730 nm). 7.5 micron thin, silver-rich emulsion on crystal-clear 100 micron polyester (PET) base (usable as B&W slide film). Resolving power 160 lp/mm at 1000:1 contrast, RMS granularity 11 (x1000). Very high exposure latitude. Perfect for the wood effect. Very suitable for B&W reversal.

stocks.name: Ortho 25 Plus
stocks.notes: Low-sensitivity orthochromatic B&W film, ISO 25/15deg, blue-green sensitivity (handleable under red darkroom safelight). Polyester (PET) base, sheet film coated on 0.180 mm / 7 mil polyester (usable as B&W slide film). Very fine grain, very high sharpness. Exposure latitude up to +/-2 f-stops (development dependent). Technical/document emulsion for reproductions, astrophotography, halftone. Extremely suitable for B&W reversal.
